species.notes: It is widely cultivated as a fruit tree and occasionally naturalized. Throughout the Philippines, in settled areas, and undoubtedly of prehistoric introduction. A native of northern India and adjoining parts of Myanmar, where it is apparently indigenous. From there, it has spread with human help to the Asiatic mainland and to many other tropical or warm subtropical regions of the world. A hybrid between Citrus hystrix × Citrus medica. Common name: lime.

species.notes: Throughout the Philippines, in most islands and provinces. In secondary and primary forests, sometimes in or near settlements, at low and medium elevation, often rather common and sometimes cultivated in home gardens and plantations, mainly for household consumption or rarely for sale in local markets. Several distinct selected forms occur in the Archipelago, with enormous variation in the size of fruits and flowers.

species.notes: The native range for citron is thought to span from the western-central Himalaya to Myanmar, with origin in the Himalayan foothills (Langgut 2017, Wu et al. 2018). Occasionally planted in the Philippines, including its cultivars, but nowhere spontaneous. The citron is the rarest of all the old cultivated citrus in the Philippines and is very seldom seen in the markets.
